behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior is algorithmic quality of the heuristic itself, not a module handoff bug. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: No OS/network/subprocess is required; fixed coordinate fixtures and known optima are enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No AI model involved. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: If route distance is computed by a separate module with a mismatch in metric assumptions, Integration could be needed; behavior text does not require that boundary, so Unit remains minimum. REJECTION-GATE: System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text only specifies route-length ratio, with no real HTTP, subprocess, or model decision dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic coordinate sets (including benchmark instances with known optimal tour length for small N and curated <=20-stop cases with precomputed optima/bounds) directly into the heuristic function and assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Real failure boundary is likely distance-provider output + road-type speed config + estimator wiring; a single-function test can miss handoff/unit-conversion bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No real OS/network/subprocess needed; in-process collaborators suffice. (b) No AI output dependency. (c) No multi-agent chain. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: If all logic is truly in one pure function, Unit would be enough; behavior text references separate inputs (distance and configured speed by road type), so module wiring is plausibly part of the failure mode. REJECTION-GATE: System is disqualified because no behavior text requires real HTTP/filesystem/process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run estimator through service-level module wiring with a local fixture route containing mixed road types and known segment distances; load test config speeds per road type; assert per-stop ETA/time-window calculations and total timeline match expected values, including unit conversions.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Behavior is a local validation rule (compare totals, compute excess), not inherently a cross-module interaction. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: No real infrastructure dependency. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: None. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: If total-time rounding policy is applied upstream, Integration might catch mismatch; behavior text scopes to validator output itself, so Unit is minimum. REJECTION-GATE: Higher levels are disqualified because text does not require external systems or model-driven decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Call validator function with table-driven cases around boundary conditions (equal to shift, slightly over, significantly over, fractional minutes) and assert rejection flag plus exact excess amount.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure appears at module boundaries: geocoding result handling -> filtering -> optimizer input -> skipped-address reporting. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) Real network is not required for this behavior; a deterministic in-process geocoder stub returning 'no results' reproduces the failure. (b) No AI output dependency. (c) No multi-agent chain. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: A real HTTP System test could reveal API-shape drift; however behavior text is specifically about 'API returns no results' handling, which is testable with contract-faithful stub payloads. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require real timeout/auth/transport conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with a fake geocoding client that returns valid coordinates for N-1 addresses and explicit empty-result payload for one address; assert optimized route excludes failed stop, includes all valid stops, and response contains the exact skipped address list.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Real failure is cross-module: dedup/aggregation preprocessing must correctly feed optimizer so route contains one visit while preserving combined items. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No OS/network/subprocess required. (b) No AI output dependency. (c) No multi-agent sequence. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: If dedup and item merge are a single pure function and optimizer is out of scope, Unit might suffice; behavior explicitly includes 'not visited twice,' which ties correctness to route-generation handoff, supporting Integration. REJECTION-GATE: System is disqualified because no real infrastructure behavior is required by text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute route-planning pipeline in-process with duplicate-address fixtures carrying distinct item sets; assert output has one stop for that address, merged item payload, and route order contains that stop exactly once.
